          capabilities:
            drop:
              - ALL                 # Removes all Linux kernel powers except the bare minimum
---

steps.name: Run Checkov Security Scan
steps.run:
checkov -d . --output json --output-file-path . || true
# checkov writes to results_*.json - rename for clarity
ls -la
find . -name "results_*.json" -exec cp {} checkov-report.json \; 2>/dev/null || \
checkov -d . --output json > checkov-report.json || true

code_block:
name: "Stage 1 · Checkov SAST Scan"
    runs-on: self-hosted
    steps:

      - name: Checkout Code
        # SHA pinned - supply chain security (prevents tag-hijacking attacks)
        uses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683

      - name: Run Checkov Security Scan
        # Scans: Terraform IaC (main.tf) + Kubernetes manifest (k8s/) + GitHub Actions YAML
        # Produces: checkov-report.json for compliance evidence
        run: |
          checkov -d . --output json --output-file-path . || true
          # checkov writes to results_*.json - rename for clarity
          ls -la
          find . -name "results_*.json" -exec cp {} checkov-report.json \; 2>/dev/null || \
          checkov -d . --output json > checkov-report.json || true

      - name: Upload Checkov Report
        # SHA pinned - upload-artifact v4.3.3
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@65462800fd760344b1a7b4382951275a0abb4808
        # if: always() ensures the report uploads even when Checkov finds issues
        if: always()
        with:
          name: checkov-scan-report
          path: checkov-report.json
          retention-days: 30

  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────
  # STAGE 2: Container vulnerability scan
  # Only runs if Stage 1 passes (security gate)
  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────
  container-security:
    name: "Stage 2 · Trivy Container Scan"

passed_checks.check_name: Suspicious use of curl with secrets

steps.name: Run Trivy Vulnerability Scanner
steps.run:
trivy image \
  --format json \
  --output trivy-report.json \
  --severity HIGH,CRITICAL \
  --exit-code 0 \
  bkimminich/juice-shop:latest

code_block:
name: "Stage 2 · Trivy Container Scan"
    runs-on: self-hosted
    needs: security-scan
    steps:

      - name: Pull Juice Shop Image
        run: docker pull bkimminich/juice-shop:latest

      - name: Run Trivy Vulnerability Scanner
        # Audit mode: exit-code 0 because Juice Shop is intentionally vulnerable
        # CRITICAL/HIGH CVEs are logged for compliance but do not block deployment
        run: |
          trivy image \
            --format json \
            --output trivy-report.json \
            --severity HIGH,CRITICAL \
            --exit-code 0 \
            bkimminich/juice-shop:latest

      - name: Upload Trivy Report
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@65462800fd760344b1a7b4382951275a0abb4808
        if: always()
        with:
          name: trivy-scan-report
          path: trivy-report.json
          retention-days: 30

  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────
  # STAGE 3: Deploy application to Kubernetes
  # Only runs if Stages 1 + 2 pass (security gate)
  # App deploys BEFORE network infrastructure (cloud-native ordering)
  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────
  app-deploy:
    name: "Stage 3 · Deploy to Kubernetes"

Wait for pod to be ready

echo "Waiting for Juice Shop pod to reach Running state..."
kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod \

kubectl get pods -o wide

code_block:
name: "Stage 3 · Deploy to Kubernetes"
    runs-on: self-hosted
    needs: container-security
    steps:

      - name: Checkout Code
        uses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683

      - name: Deploy Juice Shop to Kind Cluster
        run: kubectl apply -f k8s/juice-shop.yaml

      - name: Wait for pod to be ready
        run: |
          echo "Waiting for Juice Shop pod to reach Running state..."
          kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod \
            -l app=juice-shop \
            --timeout=120s || \
          kubectl get pods -o wide

  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────
  # STAGE 4: Provision network infrastructure
  # Runs AFTER app is deployed (correct cloud-native order:
  # cluster exists first, VPC wraps it second)
  # ─────────────────────────────────────────────
  infrastructure-deploy:
    name: "Stage 4 · Terraform Apply (LocalStack)"

steps.name: Start LocalStack if not running
steps.run:
docker ps | grep localstack | grep -v grep || \
docker run --rm -d \
  -p 4566:4566 \
  -p 4510-4559:4510-4559 \
  --name localstack \
  localstack/localstack
sleep 10
echo "LocalStack status:"
docker ps | grep localstack

code_block:
name: "Stage 4 · Terraform Apply (LocalStack)"
    runs-on: self-hosted
    needs: app-deploy
    steps:

      - name: Checkout Code
        uses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683

      - name: Start LocalStack if not running
        # Self-healing step: ensures LocalStack is always up before Terraform runs
        run: |
          docker ps | grep localstack | grep -v grep || \
          docker run --rm -d \
            -p 4566:4566 \
            -p 4510-4559:4510-4559 \
            --name localstack \
            localstack/localstack
          sleep 10
          echo "LocalStack status:"
          docker ps | grep localstack

      - name: Terraform Init
        run: terraform init

      - name: Terraform Destroy previous state
        # Idempotent: clears stale state from previous runs or VM restarts
        run: terraform destroy -auto-approve || true

      - name: Terraform Apply
        run: terraform apply -auto-approve

      - name: Verify VPC was created
        run: |
          aws --endpoint-url=http://localhost:4566 ec2 describe-vpcs \
            --query 'Vpcs[*].{ID:VpcId,CIDR:CidrBlock}' \
            --output table
